title: rhododendron tomentosum subsp. decumbens
intro: Rhododendron tomentosum subsp. decumbens, also known as the Alaskan rhododendron, is a subspecies of rhododendron that is native to the Arctic tundra regions of Alaska and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rhododendron tomentosum subsp. decumbens include its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as sore throats and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal compounds and utilize its health benefits.
